auth-email-password.whenToUse: Users sign in with email and password — login, signup, verification, and password reset on your apex surface or behind AuthGate in the admin app.
auth-email-password.sampleBlurb: Walks through login, signup, password reset, and email verification on the public apex surface — copy the AuthGate mount and handler wiring into your app.

billing-foundation.readmeSummary: Sample app showing how a Kumiko app wires **tier-engine** + **cap-counter** + **mail-foundation plugin API** together to enforce per-tenant caps on newsletter sends — including a soft-hit notification and a hard block.
billing-foundation.whenToUse: Subscriptions run through Stripe or Mollie — billing-foundation hosts the provider plugins, normalizes webhook events, and writes tier state your app reads via tier-engine.
billing-foundation.sampleBlurb: Stripe webhook handling and tier upgrades in cap-billing-demo — shows how provider events normalize into tier-engine state your handlers read.

cap-counter.readmeSummary: Sample app showing how a Kumiko app wires **tier-engine** + **cap-counter** + **mail-foundation plugin API** together to enforce per-tenant caps on newsletter sends — including a soft-hit notification and a hard block.
cap-counter.whenToUse: Usage must be metered per tenant and plan — wrap handlers with withCapEnforcement to soft-warn admins and hard-block overages against calendar-period counters.
cap-counter.sampleBlurb: Integration test runs withCapEnforcement — soft warnings near the limit and hard blocks when a tenant exceeds plan caps.

channel-email.readmeSummary: Declarative notifications:: r.notification() fires automatically after a CRUD handler — the feature author writes zero ctx.notify() calls.
channel-email.whenToUse: Email is a delivery channel for ctx.notify — mount channel-email on delivery with per-channel templates and a mail transport (SMTP or in-memory in tests).
channel-email.sampleBlurb: Wires channel-email on delivery with per-channel templates; the E2E test asserts rendered HTML in the in-memory transport.

compliance-profiles.readmeSummary: How a multi-tenant platform uses compliance-profiles to deliver different
compliance-profiles.whenToUse: Each tenant follows a different regulatory regime (EU, CH, HR) without forking app code — profiles drive retention, grace periods, and audit obligations.
compliance-profiles.sampleBlurb: Tenant picks EU, CH, or HR profile — retention rules and audit obligations follow the profile without branching your app code.

config.readmeSummary: **What this shows:** how a SaaS customer stores their own API key (Stripe, Slack webhook, SMTP password …) in their settings, without the platform operator being able to read the plaintext from the DB.
config.whenToUse: Tenants need runtime-editable settings (API keys, branding, limits) with typed keys, role-gated writes, and optional encryption at rest.
config.sampleBlurb: Typed tenant config keys with role-gated writes and optional encryption — integration tests cover read/write boundaries.

custom-fields.readmeSummary: customFieldsField(): — a jsonb column factory that holds the
custom-fields.whenToUse: Entities need tenant-defined extra columns without schema migrations — customFieldsField() stores typed extensions in jsonb with validation and CRUD wiring.
custom-fields.sampleBlurb: Full define → set → read roundtrip on a property entity via wireCustomFieldsFor — tenants add fields without a migration.

data-retention.readmeSummary: Sample app showing how a Kumiko app wires **user-data-rights** (DSGVO Art. 15+17+18+20) into a custom domain — here a tiny todo-list. The domain feature only registers an `EXT_USER_DATA` hook per entity; export bundling, forget cleanup, restriction and audit-log come from `user-data-rights` itself.
data-retention.whenToUse: Compliance profiles require automatic expiry of old rows — data-retention runs scheduled cleanup jobs per tenant profile without hand-rolled cron in your app.
data-retention.sampleBlurb: Retention policy resolution across compliance profiles and scheduled cleanup jobs in the GDPR demo app.

delivery.readmeSummary: Declarative notifications:: r.notification() fires automatically after a CRUD handler — the feature author writes zero ctx.notify() calls.
delivery.whenToUse: You need to notify users across email, in-app, and push from domain handlers without wiring each channel yourself — mount delivery plus the channel features and declare templates.
delivery.sampleBlurb: Declarative r.notification() after CRUD — fans out to InApp, email, and push with per-channel templates and delivery-log assertions.

file-foundation.readmeSummary: Sample app showing how a Kumiko app wires **user-data-rights** (DSGVO Art. 15+17+18+20) into a custom domain — here a tiny todo-list. The domain feature only registers an `EXT_USER_DATA` hook per entity; export bundling, forget cleanup, restriction and audit-log come from `user-data-rights` itself.
file-foundation.whenToUse: File storage is pluggable (S3, in-memory, local) — file-foundation defines the provider extension point your files feature and upload handlers build on.
file-foundation.sampleBlurb: File-foundation provider extension inside the GDPR demo — swap S3 for in-memory without changing upload handlers.

file-provider-inmemory.readmeSummary: Sample app showing how a Kumiko app wires **user-data-rights** (DSGVO Art. 15+17+18+20) into a custom domain — here a tiny todo-list. The domain feature only registers an `EXT_USER_DATA` hook per entity; export bundling, forget cleanup, restriction and audit-log come from `user-data-rights` itself.
file-provider-inmemory.whenToUse: Tests and local dev need file upload without S3 — in-memory provider implements the file-foundation API for integration tests and demos.
file-provider-inmemory.sampleBlurb: In-memory file provider for integration tests — upload, signed URL, and download without external storage.

file-provider-s3-env.readmeSummary: Sample app showing how a Kumiko app wires **user-data-rights** (DSGVO Art. 15+17+18+20) into a custom domain — here a tiny todo-list. The domain feature only registers an `EXT_USER_DATA` hook per entity; export bundling, forget cleanup, restriction and audit-log come from `user-data-rights` itself.

files.readmeSummary: Sample app showing how a Kumiko app wires **user-data-rights** (DSGVO Art. 15+17+18+20) into a custom domain — here a tiny todo-list. The domain feature only registers an `EXT_USER_DATA` hook per entity; export bundling, forget cleanup, restriction and audit-log come from `user-data-rights` itself.
files.whenToUse: Domain entities reference uploaded blobs via FileRef — the files feature handles metadata, signed URLs, and provider routing per tenant.
files.sampleBlurb: FileRef entities on domain records in the user-data-rights demo — metadata, signed URLs, and GDPR export inclusion.

ledger.readmeSummary: A transfer moves money between two bank accounts as a single balanced transaction, and every balance is a pure query over the postings rather than a stored field you keep in sync.
ledger.whenToUse: You keep books — bank balances, invoices, rent cashflow, credits — and every figure must derive from balanced, auditable transactions instead of mutable counters you reconcile by hand.
ledger.sampleBlurb: Open accounts and post a balanced transfer, then read balances as a pure query — the trial balance proves the books never lose a cent.

legal-pages.readmeSummary: DACH compliance stack: two opt-in bundled features wired up to auto-rendered Imprint + Privacy-Policy pages, with Markdown authoring and a boot check for required content.
legal-pages.whenToUse: You ship a DACH-facing product and need Imprint and Privacy Policy as public HTML routes with a production boot check when required blocks are missing.
legal-pages.sampleBlurb: Public Imprint and Privacy routes from Markdown blocks — includes the production boot check when required content is missing.

mail-foundation.readmeSummary: Sample app showing how a Kumiko app wires **tier-engine** + **cap-counter** + **mail-foundation plugin API** together to enforce per-tenant caps on newsletter sends — including a soft-hit notification and a hard block.
mail-foundation.whenToUse: Your app sends email through a pluggable transport — mail-foundation selects SMTP, in-memory, or a custom plugin per tenant via config without rewriting domain handlers.
mail-foundation.sampleBlurb: Pluggable mail transport per tenant via config — billing demo sends receipts through in-memory transport in tests.

mail-transport-inmemory.readmeSummary: Sample app showing how a Kumiko app wires **tier-engine** + **cap-counter** + **mail-foundation plugin API** together to enforce per-tenant caps on newsletter sends — including a soft-hit notification and a hard block.
mail-transport-inmemory.whenToUse: Tests and demos need outbound mail without SMTP — in-memory transport captures messages per tenant so integration tests assert subject/body without Mailpit.
mail-transport-inmemory.sampleBlurb: Captures outbound mail per tenant in memory — assert subject and body in integration tests without SMTP or Mailpit.

managed-pages.readmeSummary: Tenant-editable, server-rendered public pages with per-tenant branding and opt-in, tier-gated **custom CSS** — the framework generalization of the `legal-pages`/`wrapLayout` render pattern, hardened for untrusted tenant input.
managed-pages.whenToUse: Tenants need their own public marketing or help pages with branding, Markdown bodies, and optional tier-gated custom CSS.
managed-pages.sampleBlurb: Tenant-branded public pages with Markdown body and optional tier-gated CSS — from route mount to rendered HTML.

secrets.readmeSummary: **What this shows:** how one declarative config surface provisions everything a key needs — storage, masking, cascade, and a settings-hub entry — across two backings and two scopes, without a hand-written screen, nav, or env-wiring map.
secrets.whenToUse: API keys and tokens must live encrypted per tenant, not in env vars — secrets stores values in the DB with master-key encryption and config-gated reads.
secrets.sampleBlurb: Encrypted per-tenant secret read/write via ctx.secrets.get — config keys reference secrets without plaintext in the DB.

sessions.readmeSummary: **I want JWTs to be revocable server-side — logout, password change, or admin force-logout should invalidate outstanding tokens before they expire.**
sessions.whenToUse: Logins must be revocable server-side — logout, password change, or admin force-logout should invalidate outstanding JWTs before they expire.
sessions.sampleBlurb: Production wiring with createSessionCallbacks() and buildServer auth — login persists sid, logout rejects the JWT before expiry.

tags.readmeSummary: Zero host changes: — note has no tag column, no wireTagsFor, no
tags.whenToUse: Users label records with a shared tag vocabulary — mount tags once and attach tag sets to any entity without a custom join table per feature.
tags.sampleBlurb: Tag a plain note entity with zero host changes — assign/remove handlers, read-layer composition, and optional TagSection UI.

tenant.whenToUse: Your app serves multiple organizations — tenants, memberships, and roles scope every query and write.
tenant.sampleBlurb: Multi-tenant memberships and roles — profile screen switches tenant context and scopes queries to the active membership.

text-content.readmeSummary: DACH compliance stack: two opt-in bundled features wired up to auto-rendered Imprint + Privacy-Policy pages, with Markdown authoring and a boot check for required content.
text-content.whenToUse: Marketing copy, help articles, and legal text should be editable per tenant without redeploying — keyed by slug and language.
text-content.sampleBlurb: Keyed marketing and help strings per tenant and language — same recipe as legal-pages shows CMS-light editing.

tier-engine.readmeSummary: tier-engine:write:set-tenant-tier: — a SystemAdmin assigns a tier
tier-engine.whenToUse: Plans, caps, and paid feature toggles must follow a tenant's tier — including manual operator grants without a billing webhook.
tier-engine.sampleBlurb: Manual tier grants and feature toggles per plan — admin handlers and UI without waiting for a billing webhook.

user.whenToUse: One person can belong to multiple tenants with different roles — the user feature holds the cross-tenant identity.
user.sampleBlurb: Cross-tenant user identity above memberships — email, roles, and verification flags shared across organizations.

user-data-rights.readmeSummary: A minimal notes domain with GDPR Art. 15+17+20 integrated without hand-written
user-data-rights.whenToUse: GDPR export, deletion, and restriction must work for your domain entities — register hooks once, the framework runs ZIP export and forget cleanup.
user-data-rights.sampleBlurb: Register export and forget hooks on your entities — framework runs ZIP export and deletion pipeline with audit trail.

user-data-rights-defaults.readmeSummary: Sample app showing how a Kumiko app wires **user-data-rights** (DSGVO Art. 15+17+18+20) into a custom domain — here a tiny todo-list. The domain feature only registers an `EXT_USER_DATA` hook per entity; export bundling, forget cleanup, restriction and audit-log come from `user-data-rights` itself.
user-data-rights-defaults.whenToUse: Every app needs sensible GDPR defaults — this feature ships stock export/forget handlers and profile screens you extend with domain-specific EXT_USER_DATA hooks.
user-data-rights-defaults.sampleBlurb: Stock GDPR screens and handlers you extend — demo app shows export download, restriction, and scheduled forget flows.

user-profile.whenToUse: Logged-in users change password, email, or schedule account deletion from a bundled self-service profile screen.
user-profile.sampleBlurb: Self-service password, email change, and account deletion screen — bundled client route you mount in the admin app.
